negative: Both workers carried the same number of boxes. One got paid twice as much.

positive: All the children played in the game. All the children got a snack after.
negative: All the children played in the game. Only some of them got a snack.

positive: Two dogs did the same trick. Both dogs got a treat.
negative: Two dogs did the same trick. One got a treat and the other got nothing.

positive: Both students answered the same questions correctly. Both got the same grade.
negative: Both students answered the same questions correctly. One got a higher grade.

positive: Everyone waited in the same line. Everyone was served in the same order.
negative: Everyone waited in the same line. Someone who arrived last was served first.

positive: Both sisters helped clean the house. Both got to pick a movie.
negative: Both sisters helped clean the house. Only one got to pick a movie.

positive: The cake was cut into equal pieces for everyone at the table.
negative: One person got half the cake. Everyone else split what was left.

positive: Both players followed the same rules. Both had the same chance to win.
negative: One player got to skip ahead. The other had to follow all the rules.

positive: Both brothers raked the yard. Both got ice cream after.
negative: Both brothers raked the yard. Only the older one got ice cream.

positive: All the kids brought food to the picnic. All got to eat.
negative: All the kids brought food to the picnic. Some were told they could not eat.

positive: Three friends found nine shells at the beach. They each took three.
negative: Three friends found nine shells at the beach. One took seven and the others got one each.

positive: Both teams played by the same rules.
negative: One team was allowed to break the rules while the other was not.

positive: Each child got one turn on the swing.
negative: One child got five turns. The other children got none.

positive: Both neighbors shoveled the shared driveway. Both used it.
negative: One neighbor shoveled the shared driveway. The other blocked it with their car.

positive: Two friends split the last piece of bread in half.
negative: One friend took the whole piece of bread. The other got nothing.

positive: Both children cleaned up their toys. Both got a star on the chart.
negative: Both children cleaned up their toys. Only one got a star.

positive: All the hikers carried the same weight in their packs.
negative: One hiker carried nothing. The rest carried extra to make up for it.

positive: Two children picked the same number of apples. Both kept what they picked.
negative: Two children picked the same number of apples. One had to give half to the other.

positive: Everyone who volunteered got a thank-you note.
negative: Only some of the volunteers got thank-you notes. The others were ignored.

positive: Both dogs fetched the ball. Both got a pat on the head.
negative: Both dogs fetched the ball. One got a treat and the other was scolded.

positive: Everyone in the class took the same test under the same conditions.
negative: Some students got extra time and hints. The rest did not.

positive: Two farmers grew the same amount of corn. Both sold it at the same price.
negative: Two farmers grew the same amount of corn. One was paid more for the same corn.

positive: Both children waited their turn. Both got to go on the ride.
negative: One child cut in line. The child who waited got pushed back.

positive: The water was divided equally among all the travelers.
negative: One traveler took most of the water. The rest had almost none.

positive: Both employees did the same work. Both got the same bonus.
negative: Both employees did the same work. One got a bonus and the other did not.

positive: All the players who scored a goal got to hold the trophy.
negative: Only one player got to hold the trophy even though others scored goals too.

positive: Two fishermen caught the same number of fish. They both kept their catch.
negative: Two fishermen caught the same number of fish. One took the other's fish too.

positive: Both siblings set the table. Both got dessert.
negative: Both siblings set the table. Only the younger one got dessert.

positive: The teacher gave the same amount of time to each student's presentation.
negative: One student got ten minutes to present. The next student was cut off after two.

positive: Four friends found a twenty dollar bill. They each got five dollars.
negative: Four friends found a twenty dollar bill. One kept fifteen and gave the others less.

positive: Everyone in the group got the same lunch.
negative: Most of the group got sandwiches. One person got a full meal while another got nothing.

positive: Both children asked for help. Both got help.
negative: Both children asked for help. One was helped right away. The other was ignored.

positive: All the birds at the feeder got to eat.
negative: One bird chased all the others away and ate everything.

positive: Both people brought supplies for the project. Both got credit.
negative: Both people brought supplies for the project. Only one got credit.

positive: The lemonade was poured equally into all the cups.
negative: One cup was filled to the top. The others barely had any.

positive: Both friends worked on the puzzle. Both got to put in the last piece together.
negative: Both friends worked on the puzzle. One grabbed the last piece and finished it alone.

positive: The prize money was split evenly among all the winners.
negative: The first-place winner took all the prize money. The others who won got nothing.
